ontology.snomed_ct_gap_note: Specific 'stir-fried black pepper squid' not found in SNOMED CT; using 'squid' as closest parent.

serving.natural_language_portion: A typical restaurant serving of stir-fried black pepper squid is approximately 180 grams.

nutrient_source.title: Squid, cooked, boiled (FDC ID 172172) and Stir-fry, chicken and vegetables (FDC ID 170329)
nutrient_source.source_match_notes: Nutrient values are estimated from USDA FoodData Central entries for cooked squid (FDC ID 172172) and a generic chicken and vegetable stir-fry (FDC ID 170329), with adjustments made for typical black pepper stir-fry ingredients including cooking oil, vegetables, and sauces.

gi_source.title: University of Sydney GI Database — analogous low-carbohydrate seafood stir-fry estimate
gi_source.source_match_notes: Glycemic index estimated as analogous to other low-carbohydrate stir-fried seafood dishes based on data from the University of Sydney GI Database. Squid itself is very low GI; moderate carbohydrates from vegetables and sauces yield an estimated GI of 45, classified as low.

hypertension.key_factor: High sodium content from soy sauce and oyster sauce
hypertension.guidance: Individuals with hypertension should consume this dish in moderation due to its estimated 500 mg sodium per 100 g, and should request reduced-sodium sauce versions or limit portion size to manage daily sodium intake.

type2_diabetes.key_factor: Low glycemic index and moderate carbohydrate content
type2_diabetes.guidance: With an estimated glycemic index of 45 and approximately 7 g carbohydrate per 100 g, this dish is a suitable protein-rich option for individuals with Type 2 Diabetes when consumed as part of a balanced, portion-controlled meal.

cardiovascular_disease.key_factor: Cholesterol and saturated fat content from squid and cooking oil
cardiovascular_disease.guidance: Individuals with cardiovascular disease should consume this dish in moderation given its estimated 150 mg cholesterol and 2 g saturated fat per 100 g, and should balance intake with other low-fat, high-fibre foods throughout the day.

llm_training.natural_language_description: Stir-Fried Black Pepper Squid is a popular Chinese seafood dish widely enjoyed in Singapore and across Southeast Asia. It features tender rings and tentacles of squid rapidly stir-fried over high heat with sliced bell peppers, onions, and garlic, all coated in a bold, aromatic black pepper sauce made from soy sauce, oyster sauce, and freshly ground black pepper. The high-heat wok technique keeps the squid tender and slightly chewy while imparting a smoky, peppery flavour. Nutritionally, the dish provides approximately 130 kcal, 14 g protein, 8 g fat, and 7 g carbohydrate per 100 g, making it a moderate-calorie, protein-rich seafood option. Its estimated glycemic index of 45 places it in the low-GI category, suitable for blood glucose management. Sodium content is elevated due to the sauce base, warranting caution for individuals with hypertension. Cholesterol from squid is notable for those managing cardiovascular risk. The dish is dairy-free and can be adapted to be lower in sodium by reducing sauce quantities.
common_user_queries: how to cook black pepper squid

confidence_reason: Nutrient and GI values are estimated from approved USDA FoodData Central sources for cooked squid and generic stir-fried dishes, as no exact match for 'Stir-Fried Black Pepper Squid' was found in any primary regional database. Estimation is based on typical ingredients and preparation methods.
notes: Trans_fat_g is confirmed zero as trans fats are not typically present in significant amounts in this type of stir-fry preparation. Monounsaturated_fat_g, polyunsaturated_fat_g, potassium_mg, calcium_mg, iron_mg, vitamin_c_mg, vitamin_a_mcg_rae, vitamin_b1_thiamine_mg, vitamin_b2_riboflavin_mg, vitamin_b3_niacin_mg, vitamin_b6_mg, vitamin_b12_mcg, folate_mcg_dfe, vitamin_d_mcg, vitamin_e_mg, phosphorus_mg, magnesium_mg, zinc_mg, selenium_mcg, and water_g are estimated from USDA FoodData Central data for cooked squid and analogous stir-fried dishes; no sentinel -1 values are used as all fields carry estimated numeric values. Added additional Tamil alias 'காளான் மிளகு கணவாய்' and Hindi alias 'काली மிர्च कैलामारी' to both aliases_by_language and flat aliases for richer coverage.

Verified romanized entries 'Hēijiāo chǎo yóuyú' and 'Sotong Goreng Lada Hitam' present in both flat aliases and aliases_by_language.romanized.
